bids.reasoning: It is the very beginning of the negotiation (t=0.0) and the opponent has opened with an offer that yields my lowest possible utility on all issues. I will counter by asserting my ideal preferences to establish a strong starting position, proposing the maximum utility for myself across the board.

The negotiation has just begun (t=0.02) and the opponent made a small concession on my most critical issue (issueA), though their overall offer remains very low in utility. To reciprocate and signal a willingness to collaborate while keeping my high utility, I will make a small concession on my least important issue (issueD) by shifting from valueA to valueB. I am holding firm on my more important issues for now.

The negotiation is still very early (t=0.04). The opponent has been conceding on issueA, which is my most important issue, while holding firm on the other issues. Since issueA has my highest weight, I will maintain my demand for valueA while making a further small concession on my lowest-priority issue, issueD, in order to keep the negotiation moving forward and discover their preferences.

The opponent has made a significant concession by offering my most preferred value on issueA (my highest priority issue). To reciprocate while maintaining a high overall utility since it is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.06), I will hold firm on issues A, B, and C, but fully concede on issueD to valueD. This addresses what appears to be part of the opponent's core requirements while keeping my utility at a robust 0.92.

The negotiation is still very early (t=0.08), allowing me to keep my initial high aspirations. Since I previously conceded my lowest priority (issueD) to valueD (the opponent's initial bid), I will now make a small concession on my next lowest priority, issueC, shifting it to valueB. The opponent has fully conceded on my most valuable issue (issueA) and slightly budged on issueB, so I will maintain valueA for both issueA and issueB to see if they can move further towards my higher priorities.

The negotiation is still extremely early (t=0.1). The opponent has progressively conceded fully on issueA and partially on issueB (currently offering B=B), which are my highest priorities. Since they have not budged on issueC and issueD, those are likely their most valuable issues. I will hold firm on demanding maximum utility for my most important issues (issueA and issueB), but I will concede slightly more on issueC to signal a willingness to cooperate.

The opponent has made significant concessions early on by offering my highest-value options for issueA and issueB, which are my most heavily weighted issues. In their most recent bid, they offered issueA: valueA and issueB: valueA, while holding firm on issueC: valueD and issueD: valueD. I will concede fully to their valueD on issueC and make a small request for valueC on issueD, keeping my utility reasonably high at 0.787 while showing a willingness to compromise early in the negotiation.

The opponent's most recent offer `issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueD` matches an offer I made earlier (at t=0.10) and provides me with an excellent utility of 0.814. Since this is a highly favorable deal very early in the negotiation, I will accept this outcome by submitting the exact same offer to close the agreement.
